disclosure_items.clinical_fact: 6 weeks of amenorrhoea, assumed to be an irregular cycle
trigger_question_examples: When was your last menstrual period?
Is there any chance you could be pregnant?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Actually, now that you mention it, it's been about 6 weeks since my last period — I just assumed I was irregular.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Scant, dark vaginal spotting since yesterday
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any vaginal bleeding or spotting recently?
What colour is the bleeding and how much are you seeing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've had some dark spotting since yesterday.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Presyncope/near-fainting when trying to stand or walk
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt lightheaded or nearly fainted?
Do you feel your heart racing or skin clammy along with the dizziness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I nearly passed out walking to the bathroom.

trigger_question_examples: Do you have any pain in your shoulder, particularly the tip of the shoulder?
Is the shoulder pain new, and does it feel different from a muscular ache?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Actually yes, I have this odd pain right at the tip of my right shoulder too.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Could this just be a bad period cramp or a cyst?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that the combination of amenorrhoea, abnormal bleeding, severe unilateral pelvic pain, presyncope, and shoulder tip pain is highly concerning for an ectopic pregnancy, possibly with early bleeding, and requires immediate emergency assessment (000/ED) rather than being assumed to be a simple period or ovarian cyst, given the risk of life-threatening internal bleeding.
